questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Propyne was subjected to a reaction with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>HgSO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>/</mo></math> dil. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>SO</mi><mn>4</mn></msub></math>, which resulted in a product <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math>. The product <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> was heated with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ba</mi><msub><mfenced><mi>OH</mi></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msub></math> to give the product <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi></math>. The molecular formula of the product <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi></math> is
questions.solution:
When propyne is treated with dil H2SO4 in presence of HgSO4, the major product is acetone. A molecule of water is added to C=C double bond to from an enol which tautomerizes to keto form.

CH3C≡CH→HgSO4, H2SO4CH3COH=CH2CH3COH=CH2→TautomerisesCH3COCH3

The acetone formed in the above reaction, undergo aldol condensation reaction with barium hydroxide to give diacetone alcohol as shown below.

CH3COCH3⇌BaOH2CH32COHCH2COCH3CH32COHCH2COCH3→-H2O∆CH32C=CHCOCH3

The molecular formula of diacetone alcohol formed is C6H10O.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider the following reaction sequence
The correct statements about $\mathrm{Z}$ are I. It gives yellow precipitate with $\mathrm{I}_2$ and $\mathrm{NaOH}$ solution II. It undergoes disproportionation reaction in the presence of concentrated $\mathrm{NaOH}$ solution III. It undergoes wolff-Kishner reduction IV. It forms red precipitate with Fehling's reagent
questions.solution: Since ' $\mathrm{Z}$ ' is a methyl ketone, it gives positive iodoform test and gives a yellow precipitate with $\mathrm{I}_2$ and $\mathrm{NaOH}$. Aldehydes and ketones undergo walff-kishner reduction to give the corresponding hydrocarbon. Thus, I and III are correct.,
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following reactions will give benzophenone as major product?

(A) Benzoyl chloride + Benzene +AlCl3 (anhyd.)

(B) Benzoyl chloride + Phenylmagnesium bromide (excess)

(C) Benzoyl chloride + Diphenyl cadmium
questions.solution:
Reaction A involves electrophilic substitution reaction between benzene and benzoyl chloride in presence of anhydrous AlCl3 to from benzophenone.

This reaction is named as friedel crafts acylation .

Reaction B involves reaction of bezoyl chloride with phenyl magnesium bromide followed by hydrolysis.Here the product formed is triphenyl methanol.

Reaction C involves reaction of benzoyl chloride with diphenyl cadmium to form benzophenone.

Diphenyl cadmium is obtained by the reaction of cadmium chloride with phenyl magnesium bromide.

Benzophenone does not give iodoform reaction
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Consider following reaction, where

(A) The change in the functional group and

(B) The corresponding change in the hybridization from starting to the final product A and B are

CH3C≡N→i H3O+ii HI+Red Pi SnCl2,HCl
questions.solution:
Nitriles are reduced to corresponding imine with stannous chloride in the presence of hydrochloric acid, which on hydrolysis give corresponding aldehyde.

This reaction is called Stephen reaction.

When ethane nitrile undegoes stephen reduction ethanal is produced.

Ethanal on reaction with HI in presence of red phosphorous produces ethane.

The hybridization of carbon in nitrile group is sp and in ethane sp3.

The change in functional group is from -CN to -CH3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>)</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>9</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>10</mn></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math> gives a yellow coloured precipitate with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>4</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>DNP</mi></math> , but does not give red coloured precipitate with Fehling's solution. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>)</mo></math> on treatment with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>NH</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mi>OH</mi><mo>/</mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>+</mo></msup></math> gives a compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>)</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mn>9</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mn>11</mn></msub><mi>NO</mi></math>. <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, when treated with <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>PCl</mi><mn>5</mn></msub></math> , gives an isomeric compound <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>.</mo><mo> </mo><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, on hydrolysis gives propanoic acid and aniline. What will be the correct structure of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">X</mi><mo>)</mo></math>, <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Y</mi><mo>)</mo><mo> </mo></math>and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Z</mi><mo>)</mo></math>?
questions.solution:
Carbonyl compounds on reaction with 2,4-DNP gives a yellow precipitate. Hence, the compound X will be either an aldehyde or a ketone.

Compound X does not give a red precipitate with Fehling's solution. Only aliphatic aldehydes give red precipitate with Fehling's solution. Hence, the compound is not an aliphatic aldehyde.

Carbonyl compound on reaction with hydroxylamine in the presence of acid forms oxime (Y)

Oxime on reaction with PCl5 undergoes Beckmann rearrangement and forms amide.

Amide on hydrolysis gives amine and carboxylic acid.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Choose the incorrect statement among the following.
A. The reactivity of aromatic aldehydes and ketones is less than that of aliphatic carbonyl compounds towards nucleophilic addition reactions.
B. Benzaldehyde does not give Fehling's test.
C. The \(\mathrm{H}\)-atoms in ethanal are acidic in nature.
D. \(p\)-nitro benzaldehyde is less reactive than benzaldehyde towards nucleophilic addition reactions.
questions.solution:
(A) Because of the attachments of bulkier groups (aryl), the attack of nucleophile to the \(s p^2\) carbon of \( > \mathrm{C}=\mathrm{O}\) group will face steric repulsion. So, rate of the reaction will decrease. (B) Fehling's solution is being a very mild oxidising agent, benzaldehyde does not respond to it. (C) Ethanol on tautomerisation produces its enol form. It atom attacked with \(\mathrm{OH}\) group of the enol is acidic.
(D) Because of the presence of \(-\mathrm{NO}_2\) group of para position ( \(-R\) effect), the carbon atom \(\left(s p^2\right)\) of
group becomes more susceptible for nucleophilic attack as the \(s p^2\) carbon gains more partial positive charge.
So, this statement is not correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The organic compound is taken in a clean test tube and some ammonical silver nitrate solution is added to it. The mixture is then warmed. The formation of a shining silver mirror on the sides of the test tube takes place. This indicates the presence of
options: Carboxylic acid group in the organic compound
Ketone group in the organic compound
Aldehyde group in the organic compound
Ester group in the organic compound
questions.solution:
Tollens' reagent is a chemical reagent most commonly used to determine whether a known carbonyl-containing compound is an aldehyde or a ketone. It is usually ammonical silver nitrate, but can also be other mixtures, as long as aqueous diamminesilver(I) complex is present. It was named after its discoverer, the German chemist Bernhard Tollens.

A positive test with Tollens' reagent results in elemental silver precipitating out of solution, occasionally onto the inner surface of the reaction vessel, producing a characteristic and memorable "silver mirror" on the inner vessel surface.

Aldehydes will be positive in Tollen's test and a mirror-like material will be formed.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q:
In the Cannizzaro’s reaction given below :

2 Ph - CHO → KOH Ph - CH 2 OH + PhCO 2 -

The slowest step is
options: The attack of —OH at the carbonyl group
The transfer of hydride to the carbonyl group
The abstraction of proton from the carboxylic acid
questions.solution: The slowest step in Cannizzaro reaction is transfer of hydride to the carbonyl group.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statement is false?
options: Only Tollen's reagent can oxidise both aliphatic and aromatic aldehydes.
Oximes are less acidic than hydroxylamine.
Sodium borohydride does not reduce carboxyl group.
Dialkyl cadmium is considered superior to Grignard reagent for the preparation of a ketone from an acid chloride.
questions.solution: Oxime are more acidic than hydroxylamine because, there is a delocalisation of $\pi$-electrons (i.e., resonance) and it stabilises conjugate acid. While in hydroxylamine, there is no such resonance.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statements is wrong –
options: All methyl ketones give a positive iodoform test.
Acetaldehyde is the only aldehyde that gives iodoform test.
All secondary alcohols give positive iodoform test.
Any alcohol that can be oxidised to an acetyl group gives a positive iodoform test.
questions.solution: This compound which contain methyl ketonic linkage or which can change to methyl ketonic linkage can give a positive iodoform test. So, all secondary Alcohol can’t be given positive iodoform test therefore option (C) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: The reaction, <math><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mover accent="false"><mo>→</mo><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">l</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">o</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">i</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">e</mi></mrow></mover><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi></math> is called-
questions.solution: It is Tishchenko reaction, any aldehyde with or without alpha hydrogen undergoes disproportionation in the presence of aluminium ethoxide to give ester.

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
How many among the following compounds will give the above result?
i. Cyclohexanone
ii. Acetone
iii. Propionaldehyde
iv. Acetophenone
v. Acetaldehyde
vi. Benzophenone
vii. Benzaldehyde
questions.solution:
Positive 2,4-DNP test ⇒ Carbonyl group. Positive Fehling's test ⇒ Aliphatic aldehyde group. Aromatic aldehydes and ketones do not give Fehling's test. Hence, among the given options, compounds
(iii) and (v) will give positive 2,4-DNP and Fehling's test.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

The deprotonation of <math><mi mathvariant="normal">p</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi><mo>-</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></math>

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following ester can be used as acylating agent in mixed Claisen condensation?
questions.solution:
A cross Claisen Condensation or mixed Claisen Condensation is a condensation reaction between two different esters. Like a cross aldol addition, a cross Claisen Condensation is a useful reaction only if it is carried out under conditions that foster the formation of primarily one product. Otherwise, the reaction will form a mixture of products that are difficult to separate.

Primarily, one product will be formed from the cross Claisen Condensation if one of the esters has no α-hydrogen (and therefore cannot form an enolate ion) and the ester with α-hydrogen is added slowly to a solution of the ester without α-hydrogen and the alkoxide ion.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
